content.problemStatement:
Maximum Sum of subarray of size K    (2.5 Marks)Given an integer array arr of size N and an integer K, find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size K.

A subarray is a contiguous part of the array.


Input Format
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases.


For each test case:


The first line contains two integers N and K.


The second line contains N space-separated integers representing the array elements.



Output Format
For each test case, print the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size K
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and K (1 ≤ K ≤ N). The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size K.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The subarray of size 3 with maximum sum is [5,1,3] whose sum is 9.
All subarrays of size 2 have sums -3, -5, -7; the maximum is -3.
The last three elements 4,5,6 give the maximum sum 15.

solution.approach: Use a sliding‑window of length K. First compute the sum of the first K elements. This is the current window sum and also the best answer so far. Then slide the window one position to the right by subtracting the element that leaves the window and adding the new element that enters. After each slide update the best answer if the new window sum is larger. The process visits each element a constant number of times, giving linear time. No extra arrays are needed, only a few variables of type long long (or long) to store sums.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case – each element is added and removed at most once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space – only a few scalar variables are used irrespective of N.

code.Python3:
import sys
def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        cur = sum(arr[:K])
        best = cur
        for i in range(K, N):
            cur = cur - arr[i-K] + arr[i]
            if cur > best:
                best = cur
        out.append(str(best))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
